content.full_text:
California
Fair POlitical
Practices Cornrnission
August 6, 1,990
Carole BaiIey
capistrano Unified School oistrict
32972 calle Perfecto
san Juan capistrano, cA 92675
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our Fil-e No. A-9O-43 6
Dear Ms. Bailey:
John tlcLean, Legal- Counsel with the Commission,s Enforcement
Division, has asked me to respond to your June 14, L99O, ]etter
concerning the carnpaign provisions of the Political 
Reform Act.1
OUESTION
Are payments made by the capistrano Unified School District
(CUSD) , as itemized in Jerorne R. Thornsley,s letter dated January
31, 1990, to Michael Kenney, reportable under the Act?
CONCLUSION
The paynents for item 1 are not reportable under the Act.
However, the pa)rments for items 2-9 are reportable as independent
expend5.tures. (Mr. Thornsley's letter is enclosed for your
convenience . )
FACTS
The capistrano Unif j-ed School District held an el,ection on
llay 9, 1989, to form a MeIIo-Roos Facilities District 
(Measure A)
CUSD incurred expenses in relation to holding the election,
including hiring consultants, producing and distributing
inforrnational material, and establishing phone lines for the
purpose of contacting voters '
1 
co.r"arrment Code Sections 81OOO-91015. AIl statutory
references are to the Government code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18OOO; et seq. efl references to regulations are to Title
2, Divj.sion 6 of the California Code of Regulations.
.t2ti J strcet, suitc 8or) o P.O. Box st'\7 O Sacramcnro CA 958r).l-Otlo7 .) (916) )ZZ-366L)


Carole Bailey
Page Two
ANALYSIS
Section 82013 (b) defines a rrconmitteeir as any person or
combinatj.on of persons tho directly or indirectly Dakes
independent expenditures totaling $1,OOO in a calendar year.
An independent expenditure is defined as:
. . . an expenditure nade by any person in connection with a
cornrnunication which expresslv advocates the election or
defeat of a clearly identified candidate or the
qualification, 
passage or defeat of a clearly identified
neasure, or taken as a whole and in context. unarnbiquously
urqes a particular result in an election but which is not
made to or at the behest of the affected candidate or
committee.
(Section 8203L, emphasis added.)
Section 84200(b) requires all independent expenditure
committees to file campaign statenents by JuIy 31 for the period
ending June 30, and January 31 for the period ending December 31
if they have made independent expenditures during the six-nonth
period before the closing date of the statements. In addition,
depending upon the independent expenditure committee,s actiwities,
'r1ate independent expenditure reportstr may be required.
A late independent expenditure is:
...an independent expenditure whicb totals in the aggregate
$1,OOO or nore and is made for or against any specific
candidate or neasure involved in an election before the date
of the election but after the closing date of the Last
campaign statenent required to be filed prior to the election
by a candidate or conrnittee participating in such election.
(Section 82035.5. )
In other words, if the independent expenditure committee made
independent expenditures which totalled $1,o00 during the 16 days
before the May 9, L989, election, a late independent expenditure
report was due within 24 hours of making the expenditures. (see
page 23 of the enclosed Tnformation }ilanual E: campaiqn Provisions
of the PoliticaL Reforn Act for Maior Donor Conrnittees and
Independent Expenditure committees for instructions on cornpleting
reports.) 
Even though the election has passed, if late
independent expenditures were made, the commj.ttee is still
required to fite the late independent exPenditure rePorts. .
However, the reports nay be sent by regular first class mail and
need not be sent by guaranteed overnight


carole BaiIey
Page Three
mail, as normally required.
copy enclosed. )
(Pessner Advice Letter, No. A-87-263,
In reference to Mr. Thornsley's letter, costs paid to the
orange county Registrar of voters to conduct the election, item L,
did not constitute an independent expenditure. However, itens 2
through 9 aII pertain to expenses associated with cornmunications
which expressly advocated or, taken as a whole and in context,
unambj-guously urged passage of ueasure A. Although Jerome R.
Thornsley's letter of January 31, 1990, to Michael Kenney ot dhe
Fair Potitical Practices comnission indicated that rrno printed or
oral conmunication urged a rryesrr vote by potential voters,rt the
material-s provided to John c. l{cLean of the Commj.ss j.on by your
office state otherwise. As an exanple, the last page of Exhibit A
states:
Those who advocate a tryesrr vote on Measure A are encouraged
to join in the effort to urge voters to approve this measure.
And Exhibit D, a flyer sent to Laguna Niguel and Dana Point
voters reads, in part:
There's a lot in Dleasure A for Laguna Niguel and Dana Point
residents. 
Measure A -- t{ay 9
Therefore, since the total oi independent expenditures made
in items 2 through 9 is 9r,0o0 or more, and presumably were made
within one calendar year, CUSD qualified as an independent
expenditure comrnittee and must f il,e a campaign statenent for each
six month period (January 1 through June 30 or July 1 through
December 31) in which independent expenditures were nade, in
addition to any late independent expenditure reports that were
required. These reports must be filed vith the orange county
Registrar's office as soon as possible. A copy of the filed
reports should be forwarded to lilr. ucLean,
If you have any additional questions, please contact me at
(916) 322-5662.
Sincerely,
Enc Iosures
Politi.ca1 Reform consultant
?il4,*,--
